steps.title: This protocol is adapted from Stobart, et al
steps.instruction: This protocol is adapted from Stobart, et al. 2017 and BEI Resources (see References tab) Note: This protocol assumes the user is familiar with cell culture techniques and transfection procedures. It’s always a good idea to use pSynkRSV(mKate2) plasmid as a positive control if rescuing adapted strains (Confirm these plasmids with someone who knows them, or verify by whole plasmid sequencing before you use them). Initial cell culture: Routine sub-passage BSR T7/5 cells in tissue culture medium +Geneticin (500 μg/mL) as a growth medium. Add the Geneticin (500 μg/mL) to the growth medium every passage.

steps.title: Prepare 6 well plates for transfection...
steps.instruction: Prepare 6 well plates for transfection from T75 flask one night before transfection. Aspirate the growth medium from the flask. Add 2 mL of trypsin-EDTA and rock flasks to distribute the trypsin-EDTA and incubate at 37 °C 5% CO 2 for about 00:02:00 . When cells start to dislodge from the flask, add 8 mL of tissue culture medium to the flask and use a pipet to suspend the cells in this growth medium. Count the cells and put 4.5x10 5 cells in one well of the 6-well plate. Incubate the plates at 37 °C 5% CO 2 in the tissue culture incubator.

steps.title: Use a 3:1 ratio of Lipofectamine...
steps.instruction: Use a 3:1 ratio of Lipofectamine (μL) to plasmid/helper plasmid (μg).

steps.title: Dilute each component with Opti-MEM to...
steps.instruction: Dilute each component with Opti-MEM to make 100 µL of each. After dilution, allow each dilution to sit at room temperature for 00:05:00 . Use the following amounts of each component per transfection: a) RSV antigenome (NR-
0.8 μg (8 μL of 0.1 μg/μL ) + 92 μL Opti-MEM b) pA2-Lopt, L protein (NR-
0.2 μg (2 μL of 0.1 μg/μL) + 98 μL Opti-MEM c) pA2-Nopt, N protein (NR-
0.4 μg (4 μL of 0.1 μg/μL) + 96 μL Opti-MEM d) pA2-Popt, P protein (NR-
0.4 μg (4 μL of 0.1 μg/μL) + 96 μL Opti-MEM e) pA2-M2-1opt, M2-1 protein (NR-
0.4 μg (4 μL of 0.1 μg/μL) + 96 μL Opti-MEM f) Lipofectamine 2000 6.6 μL + 93.4 μL Opti-MEM For multiple transfections increase the above quantities proportionally.

steps.title: After allowing the diluted components to...
steps.instruction: After allowing the diluted components to sit at room temperature for 00:05:00 , combine all six components in one vial, mix gently and incubate the transfection mixture at room temperature for 00:20:00 .

steps.title: Transfection mixtures should be 600 µL...
steps.instruction: Transfection mixtures should be 600 µL total (Opti-MEM, Lipofectamine, and DNA). Aspirate the media from the BSR T7/5 cell culture plate, wash cells twice with 1 mL Opti-MEM for each wash, and aspirate the final wash.

steps.title: Add 600 µL transfection mixture to...
steps.instruction: Add 600 µL transfection mixture to each well and incubate the plate for 02:00:00 at room temperature on a shaker/rocker plate set at low speed.

steps.title: After 2 hours, add an additional...
steps.instruction: After 2 hours, add an additional 600 µL Opti-MEM per well and place the plate in a 37 °C 5% CO 2 tissue culture incubator overnight (8-12 hours).

steps.title: After overnight incubation Aspirate and discard...
steps.instruction: After overnight incubation Aspirate and discard the transfection mixture from the wells. Wash each well once with 1 mL sterile PBS. Aspirate the PBS and replace with 2 mL of infection medium - RSV per well ( Note : no Geneticin is added in the medium post-transfection). Continue incubation at 37 °C 5% CO 2 in the tissue culture incubator overnight.

steps.title: Monitor flasks for cytopathic effect (CPE)...
steps.instruction: Monitor flasks for cytopathic effect (CPE) and sub-pass transfected cells into T25/T75 flasks when confluent (approximately every 48 hours). CPE shows first as mini-syncytia and then grows into rounded up clumps of cells. Cells should remain in infection medium - RSV throughout the rest of the rescue.

steps.title: When CPE is evident throughout >50%...
steps.instruction: When CPE is evident throughout >50% of the flask, which typically is ~7 days post transfection (mutants may require more or less time), scrape the cells into the growth media and aliquot into cryovials following these steps: Scrape the T75 flasks and transfer contents to 50 mL tube Spin at 1180 rpm for 00:05:00 Remove the majority of the media and place on ice, resuspend the cell pellet in about 2.5 mL of the media Freeze/thaw pellet 3 times, with vortexing between each round Spin at 1180 rpm for 00:05:00 Combine the media from the freeze/thaw with the media placed on ice Aliquot into tubes, snap freeze, and place at -80 °C or colder. -This is P0 of the stock-
